title: Harbour hoist
description: Use resultant force to diagnose and repair a harbour hoist.

audience.summary: GCSE learners who can add signed whole numbers.

skills.title: Calculate a resultant force
skills.description: Add forces with direction to decide whether the resultant is zero and identify its direction.

skills.title: Choose an evidence based repair
skills.description: Use an observed force imbalance and its reading to select a causal repair.

campaigns.title: Harbour hoist campaign
campaigns.description: Investigate an imbalance in a harbour hoist, then repair it using force evidence.

reviewItems.title: Resultant force on a hoist
reviewItems.standaloneContext: A hoist platform has a 120 N upward force and a 90 N downward force. Treat upward as positive.
reviewItems.prompt: What is the resultant force on the platform?

feedback.success: The signed sum is $+120\,\text{N} - 90\,\text{N} = +30\,\text{N}$, so the resultant is 30 N upward.
misconceptions: Zero would require equal opposing forces.
Adding magnitudes without keeping their directions produces 210 N, not the resultant.
hints: Choose one direction as positive before adding the forces.
